visual_evidence: Human asks from mixed docs; assistant workspace turns it into shared outputs for multiple coworkers; strongest cue is collaboration/team handoff rather than IDE or PR automation.
semantic_comment: Reads as collaborative assistant embedded with a human team, not specifically terminal or autonomous PR workflow.

visual_evidence: End-to-end board with issue/task intake, repo tree, code editor, file diffs, checks, security, review/status panels, and completed workflow outputs.
semantic_comment: Best match for Devin because it shows autonomous work from task handoff through code/files/tests/review completion.

visual_evidence: Large IDE window dominates: file tree, code editor, inline selection/suggestion, side panels, and agent inside the editor surface.
semantic_comment: The visual language is editor-first, which fits Cursor more than terminal-centric Claude Code or broader Codex orchestration.

visual_evidence: Claude-like mark appears; workflow centers on code files, review checklist, and developer handoff, with code/tool icons rather than a full IDE product frame.
semantic_comment: Likely Claude Code: coding assistant workflow with Claude branding, though it is less terminal-centric than ideal.

visual_evidence: Visible GitHub Copilot wordmark; repo graph, branch/check workflow, code suggestion card, and team review imagery.
semantic_comment: Directly branded as GitHub Copilot and semantically tied to repo/code suggestions.

visual_evidence: Broad agent orchestration dashboard with flowchart, code panel, documents, security checks, metrics, and robot co-pilot across the process.
semantic_comment: Fits Codex as general code-agent orchestration; lacks the explicit PR/review completion emphasis that would make it Devin.
